reason: Round 2 ADR.md resolves the sole medium gap from round 1. ADR-015 correctly identifies FK CASCADE vs application-layer tension, invokes GDPR Art.17(3)(d) for security_logs, specifies SAVEPOINT-based idempotent recovery for FR-92 30-day SLA, adds NP-15 forced integration test. All 15 ADRs structurally compliant. Three surviving low gaps are acceptable.
citations: ADR.md ADR-015: Decision — application-layer ordered erasure, SAVEPOINT per table
ADR.md ADR-015: Rationale — FK CASCADE violates GDPR Art.17(3)(d) security log retention
ADR.md ADR-015: Consequences — NP-15 forced for partial-failure recovery path
ADR.md ADR-001: Acceptance criteria — cyclic-import + CRG cohesion ≥0.38
ADR.md ADR-014: pii:none explicit prohibition in ROLE_PERMISSIONS

gaps.message: ADR-002 and ADR-003 omit explicit acceptance criteria (latency thresholds stated in consequences but not as formal criteria block)
ADR-015 defers FK constraint handling to 'temporarily deferred' without specifying SET CONSTRAINTS DEFERRED vs ALTER TABLE — implementer must infer
FR-60/62 RBAC enforcer decorator pattern undocumented — covered implicitly by ADR-014 but no dedicated ADR
